classification.subject: normalized state and finite distribution
classification.inputScope: one normalized state or two distributions
classification.observationScope: full computational-basis distribution
classification.ancillaPolicy: none

declarations.finalDisposition: Pointwise computational-basis weights of normalized states are exactly equality of their packaged finite distributions.
declarations.goal3Boundary: Vector, ray, all-pure-input, and channel equality remain strictly stronger or incomparable claims.

classification.subject: finite distributions
classification.inputScope: one pair of pointwise-equal distributions
classification.observationScope: finite events and deterministic finite postprocessing
classification.ancillaPolicy: deterministic map supplies classical marginal/decoder

declarations.finalDisposition: Pointwise finite-distribution equality yields all finite-event equalities and equality after every deterministic finite pushforward.
declarations.goal3Boundary: Randomized postprocessing, quantum partial trace, and channel equality are outside this family.

classification.subject: matrix action on state
classification.inputScope: every compatible state under a supplied unitary
classification.observationScope: norm, total weight, normalization
classification.ancillaPolicy: none

declarations.finalDisposition: This is forward normalization-preserving support for supplied unitary evolution, not equality of pre- and post-evolution behavior.
declarations.goal3Boundary: Converse classifications of norm preservers remain in EQC-045 and Goal 3.

classification.space: cross: complex matrix/column to doubled real matrix/column
classification.subject: rectangular matrix action on encoded raw state columns
classification.inputScope: every raw complex source column and every raw coefficient pair in ℝ × ℝ
classification.observationScope: exact equality of every evolved target amplitude after encoding
classification.ancillaPolicy: explicit doubled sum coordinates and coefficient pair; no normalized or product-top interpretation

declarations.finalDisposition: Every raw real coefficient pair and source column satisfies exact cross-space matrix/state intertwining.
declarations.goal3Boundary: Raw coordinate coefficients are not normalized product ancillas, rays, densities, or decoded outcomes.

declarations.goal3Boundary: This one-input strictness witness is not channel or all-measurement inequivalence.

classification.subject: ordered circuits
classification.inputScope: every chronological list
classification.observationScope: exact operator denotation
classification.ancillaPolicy: none

declarations.finalDisposition: Ordered circuits are exactly equal precisely when their chronological evaluator matrices are literally equal; gatewise and append congruence are proved.
declarations.goal3Boundary: Gate-list equality is sufficient but unnecessary; phase, resources, schedules, and cross-model maps remain separate.

classification.subject: two legal schedules of one occurrence family
classification.inputScope: all supplied schedules under pairwise-commuting global denotations
classification.observationScope: exact evaluated operator
classification.ancillaPolicy: no schedule selection; same finite occurrence family

declarations.finalDisposition: Any two supplied legal schedules are exactly circuit-equal under pairwise commutation of distinct occurrence denotations.
declarations.goal3Boundary: No converse and no generic disjoint-support implication without scalar commutation are claimed.

classification.space: same quaternionic source space plus exact cross-space complexification clauses
classification.subject: two legal schedule circuits and evaluators, their exact complex translations, a fixed normalized ground input, and normalized pure inputs
classification.inputScope: fixed normalized ground input for all-basis-weight equality; one normalized interference input witnesses failure of all normalized pure input equality
classification.observationScope: source and translated ExactCircuitEq failure; fixed ground-input all-basis-weight equality; failure of PureInputBasisMeasurementEq; one translated decoded bottom-weight inequality
classification.exactness: exact equality and exact counterexamples at the stated scopes
classification.ancillaPolicy: source clauses have no ancilla; translated clauses use the explicit complexification AddedWire and canonical encoded input, with no product or mixed-top claim

declarations.finalDisposition: The source schedules and exact complex translations are operator-distinct; one fixed normalized ground input agrees in all basis weights, but equality over all normalized pure inputs fails, as witnessed by a separate normalized interference input.
declarations.goal3Boundary: This does not assert channel, all-effect, or every-input inequivalence.

classification.space: cross: complex circuit output to decoded doubled-real output
classification.subject: raw and normalized translated-circuit outcome behavior at four separate levels
classification.inputScope: all raw point weights without unitarity; normalized distributions, finite events, and deterministic pushforwards under local unitarity and normalized source/top data
classification.observationScope: decoded point weights, full finite distribution, every finite event, and deterministic finite pushforwards

declarations.goal3Boundary: Mixed top states, randomized postprocessing, partial trace, and channel equality remain outside this result.
